description:
A deployment engineer left you two files under
    /home/user/deployment/

      • /home/user/deployment/schema.json  
      • /home/user/deployment/updates.json  

    1. schema.json is a Draft-07 JSON-Schema that formally describes a
       single “update” record and enforces:
          • a required string property “update_id”  
          • a required string property “version” that must follow the
            semantic-version pattern “MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH”  
          • a required string property “status” whose value must be either
            “pending” or “applied”  
          • no additional properties are allowed.  

    2. updates.json is an array containing five candidate update
       descriptors that must be validated against that schema before the
       engineer can continue with the rollout.

    Your task as the on-call SRE is to perform a quick pre-flight check
    entirely with a single jq command and produce a plain-text log called

        /home/user/deployment/invalid_updates.log

    The log MUST follow **exactly** the format below so that automated
    pipelines can parse it:

        • One line per invalid element in the order they appear in
          updates.json.  
        • Each line must be literally the string:
              INVALID: <update_id>
          where “<update_id>” is the exact value of the offending
          object’s update_id field.  
        • The file must terminate with a single trailing newline and no
          extra blank lines.  

    You do not need to modify the original JSON files.  
    Do not move or rename any files or directories.  
    Use only user-level privileges.  
    Hint: You can pipe updates.json into jq, inspect each element and
    emit the update_id when any of the three schema rules is violated.
truth:
Initial file tree and contents that already exist when the task starts:

    Directory: /home/user/deployment/

    File: /home/user/deployment/schema.json
    -------------------------------------------------
    {
      "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
      "title": "Update descriptor",
      "type": "object",
      "required": ["update_id", "version", "status"],
      "properties": {
        "update_id": { "type": "string" },
        "version": {
          "type": "string",
          "pattern": "^(0|[1-9][0-9]*)\\\\.(0|[1-9][0-9]*)\\\\.(0|[1-9][0-9]*)$"
        },
        "status": {
          "type": "string",
          "enum": ["pending", "applied"]
        }
      },
      "additionalProperties": false
    }
    -------------------------------------------------

    File: /home/user/deployment/updates.json
    -------------------------------------------------
    [
      {"update_id":"udp-100","version":"1.2.0","status":"pending"},
      {"update_id":"udp-101","version":"1.2","status":"pending"},
      {"update_id":"udp-102","version":"1.3.0","status":"deploying"},
      {"update_id":"udp-103","version":"1.4.0","status":"applied"},
      {"update_id":"udp-104","version":"1.5.0"}
    ]
    -------------------------------------------------

    What the agent must create:

    File: /home/user/deployment/invalid_updates.log
    -------------------------------------------------
    INVALID: udp-101
    INVALID: udp-102
    INVALID: udp-104

    -------------------------------------------------
    (note: one trailing newline after the last line)

    The automated checker will:
      1. Verify that /home/user/deployment/invalid_updates.log exists.  
      2. Verify that its exact content, including newlines, matches the
         block shown above.  
      3. Ensure no extra or missing lines are present.
